pattern: Recursively decompose the string into its top-level balanced special chunks, maximise each chunk's interior the same way, then sort the chunks in descending order and concatenate to get the lexicographically largest arrangement.
hints: Scan with a running balance of ones minus zeros; each time it returns to zero you have isolated one top-level special substring.
Every such chunk looks like 1 + (inner special string) + 0; recursively maximise that inner part before doing anything else.
Because swapping adjacent special substrings is allowed, you may reorder the top-level chunks freely; sort the maximised chunks in descending lexicographic order and join them.

edge_inputs.description: swap rearranges into the largest form
two equal chunks need no reordering
deeper nesting recurses inside a chunk
smaller chunk should move after larger

stress.description: random valid special strings built by balanced construction
stress.note: construction guarantees the string is special (balanced, valid prefixes)
